macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Stock Markets Mixed as Tech Sector Declines
items.summary: The Nasdaq fell 1.18% to 22,878, S&P 500 declined 0.54% to 6,908, while Dow edged up 0.03% to 49,499 amid tech sell-off despite Nvidia's strong earnings. Investor sentiment turned cautious on AI capex concerns and valuations.

items.title: US Stock Futures Slide as Nvidia Drops 5-6%
items.summary: U.S. stock futures are lower with S&P 500 futures down 0.2% and Dow futures off 0.4%, following Thursday's mixed session where Nasdaq dropped 1.18% and S&P fell 0.54%. Focus remains on corporate earnings.

items.title: European Stocks and US Futures Fall Amid Earnings Watch
items.summary: European Stoxx 600 down 0.1%, FTSE 100 up 0.1%, CAC 40 and DAX down 0.2%; US futures for S&P 500 -0.2%, Dow -0.4%. Asian markets mixed with Nikkei +0.2%, Hang Seng +0.9%; oil prices rose.

items.title: Treasury Yields Dip as Investors Rotate to Bonds
items.summary: US 2-year yield fell 4.3 bps to 3.428%, 10-year to 4.004%; German 10-year Bund steady at 2.697%. US dollar strengthened modestly with DXY up 0.18% to 97.81.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Global Cryptocurrency Market Cap Reaches $2.35T with 4.08% Daily Gain
items.summary: The global cryptocurrency market capitalization has climbed to $2.35 trillion, marking a 4.08% increase over the past 24 hours according to CoinMarketCap data.

items.title: Bitcoin Rebounds to $68,000 After Brief Dip to $62,000; Derivatives Markets Signal Caution
items.summary: Bitcoin broke out of a low-volatility range after falling to $62,000, rebounding sharply toward $68,000. However, derivatives markets continue to show cautious sentiment with perpetual futures open interest declining and options markets pricing in downside risk.

items.title: Bitcoin ETF Outflows Continue; Fifth Consecutive Monthly Decline Looms
items.summary: Spot Bitcoin ETFs have recorded net outflows for four consecutive months, putting Bitcoin on track for its fifth straight monthly decline—a pattern last seen during the 2018 bear market. Ethereum spot ETFs are similarly approaching a fourth month of net outflows.

items.title: Crypto Winter Persists as Market Follows Technology Stock Sentiment
items.summary: The cryptocurrency market continues to experience a crypto winter, with market trends closely following the sentiment of technology stocks amid broader economic uncertainty.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: New Automated Pipeline Detects Hidden 'Unverbalized' Biases in Large Language Models
items.summary: Researchers from University of Buenos Aires, University College London, Imperial College London, and independent researchers introduce an automated pipeline using LLM 'autoraters' to expose subtle biases in LLMs that influence decisions without explicit mention in reasoning chains. The method generates bias concepts from datasets, tests them statistically, and confirms adaptability across bias studies.

items.title: PaperQA3 Released as Frontier Multimodal Deep Research Agent for Science
items.summary: PaperQA3 introduces top-performing multimodal capabilities for deep research agents, enabling reading of figures and tables from over 150M papers and patents, with an updated open-source algorithm outperforming current frontier agents on benchmarks.

items.title: New IT Roles Emerge for Continuous AI Evaluation Amid Rise of Autonomous Agents
items.summary: Organizations are forming AI evaluation teams as essential safety nets for deploying complex AI agents, addressing surprising outputs and non-deterministic behaviors beyond initial tests, with experts emphasizing ongoing human oversight alongside tools.

items.title: Macron Positions France-India as 'Third Force' in AI at New Delhi Summit
items.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron advocates France and India as a sovereign alternative in the global AI race beyond U.S.-China dominance, while highlighting Europe's quantum ambitions with four national champions in quantum computing platforms.

items.title: Pasqal Delivers 140-Qubit Neutral Atom QPU to Italy's CINECA Supercomputing Center
items.summary: Pasqal ships a 140-qubit neutral atom quantum processing unit to CINECA in Bologna, enhancing Italy's public supercomputing capabilities as part of the ICSC high-performance computing and quantum initiative.

items.title: Welinq and Pasqal Strengthen Collaboration for Networked Neutral-Atom Quantum Computing
items.summary: Welinq and Pasqal advance joint efforts to develop scalable, network-ready quantum architectures using interconnected neutral-atom processors for data center deployment.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: GameSquare Expands Partnership with Capcom for Resident Evil Requiem Launch
items.summary: GameSquare Holdings announced a new campaign partnership with Capcom to support the global launch of Resident Evil Requiem on February 27, 2026, targeting fans across digital, creator, and real-world channels.[1][4]

items.title: Pokemon Presents Livestream Set for February 27 Celebrating 30 Years
items.summary: The Pokemon Presents event airs February 27 at 6 AM PT / 9 AM ET on Nintendo's YouTube channel, expected to reveal next mainline Pokemon games, GBA re-releases like FireRed and LeafGreen, and updates for live-service titles.[2]

items.title: Porsche Partners with Capcom for Resident Evil Requiem Featuring Cayenne Turbo GT
items.summary: Porsche and Capcom collaborated to feature a custom Cayenne Turbo GT show car in Resident Evil Requiem, launching February 27, with a short film starring Leon's voice actor premiering earlier.[5]

items.title: Netflix Acquisition of Warner Bros. Games Reportedly Off the Table
items.summary: Plans for Netflix to acquire Warner Bros. Games appear canceled, following Warner Bros. Discovery's scheduled approval meeting on March 20, 2026, amid competition from Paramount.[3]

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Global Smartphone Market Projected to Shrink 13% in 2026 Due to Memory Chip Shortage
items.summary: IDC forecasts a sharp decline in smartphone shipments to 1.1 billion units in 2026 from 1.26 billion the prior year, driven by an unprecedented memory chip crunch affecting production. The shortage has led to downside revisions in projections, with pressure building into February.

items.title: Samsung and SK Hynix Positioned as Winners Amid Memory Chip Market Shifts
items.summary: Samsung and SK Hynix are expected to benefit from the memory chip dynamics, with Kioxia (SanDisk) reporting strong earnings outlook of up to $5.2 billion operating income and $13 billion revenue, partnering with Nvidia on next-gen NAND for AI data centers.

items.title: Netflix Exits Bid for Warner Bros., Paving Way for Paramount Deal
items.summary: Netflix has dropped out of the bidding for Warner Bros., allowing Paramount to potentially clinch the studio acquisition, leading to modest gains for Paramount shares while Warner Bros. softens.
